programme.desc: A broken coffee table requires expertise to redeem it, bag-maker Neil Wragg uses all of his creativity to rethink an old bicycle and Sarah Moore renovates an old dressing table(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper head to East Sussex for their next auction, and once again Raj gambles a three-figure sum on an item while Margie tries to be clever with her purchases(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: David Dimbleby travels across the UK in the lead up to the 2019 general election to reveal why it is going to be one of the most unpredictable elections in recent history. David finds a United Kingdom divided, and discovers people's views on Brexit have largely hardened, while party allegiances have weakened, and fury with politicians is rarely far from the surface(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam Frisby signs up social media influencer and body positivity campaigner Chessie King to work on a new collection for In The Style. Adam wants the new collection to be released before the summer season ends, which means the team must work quickly to get their ideas together, design and make the range(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste reveal how scammers were able to con one woman out of her money not once, but twice(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a house in Brierley Hill in the West Midlands, a former bank in Hyde, Greater Manchester, and a terraced home in Stanley Grove in London(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A motorist is caught trying to claim for the same damage twice, and a holiday-maker's claim for lost luggage is grounded thanks to forged documents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop assistant is terrified when armed robbers burst in, while police try to catch a carer who steals money from elderly victims(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Tim Weeks and Colin Young help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of York. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Clifton Park Museum in Rotherham(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi and Al realise they have forgotten an important date. Valerie has to think on her feet when she tries to help an elderly patient about to be conned out of her life savings(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets a couple looking for a sanctuary in the Lancashire countryside for themselves and some donkeys. He also visits the Helmshore Mills Textile Museum(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is joined by Norman Wilkinson and Daniel Heath, who between them revamp a guest bed, a tired armchair and an artist's easel - turning the latter into a vanity unit(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper hunt for items along the West Sussex coast, taking in a Sherman tank, a Jack Russell and a mannequin on their way to the auction in Winchester(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: A live report from Holyhead on the start of this year's Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need, as a team of six young people, supported by Matt Baker, set out on an eight-day journey. The team will travel across Wales for three days before crossing over into England in Oswestry, and then making their way down to the finish line at the BBC Elstree Studios on Friday November 15 during the Children in Need appeal show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany cannot contain her excitement when she learns the register office has a cancellation later that day so she, Keegan and Bernadette make final preparations for a trip to Scotland. But unknown to them, Louise takes great pleasure in telling Karen their plans. Ruby is thrilled when Sharon finally signs over the E20, and Ian is determined to make his campaign for Walford councillor a success - unknown Iqra is plotting against him. Pam arrives back in Walford, so Jay and Lola hatch a plan to surprise Ben, who is thrilled to see her(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: Officers prepare for a visit from President Trump - and for keeping in line the protestors who are out in force. In south London, a 38-year-old man is violently attacked on a bus and rushed to hospital in a critical condition. Due to the serious nature of the attack, the murder detectives are brought in to assist - and as they scour CCTV footage, they see the suspect leaving a vital clue as he makes his getaway. Response officers Ben and Tom assist in the eviction of a traveller from a supermarket car park in Croydon(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Glasgow, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present a combination of gossip and serious analysis from Westminster and Brussels(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate how technology enables people to be caught out by elaborate online romance fraud(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A railway worker's £300,000 personal injury claim for a broken thumb hits the buffers when undercover filming proves he is taking his insurers for a ride, and a fraudulent claim for a mobile phone lost in a river sinks like a stone when the handset resurfaces in a recycling shop. Ore Oduba presents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An elderly man's home is broken into and expensive watches of great sentimental value are taken. Thankfully, a neighbour's security cameras video the thief in the act. Plus, a prolific burglar targets businesses across a whole area, including a pizza shop, a pub and a chippy. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma is furious to discover Heston's memorial bench has been stolen and resolves to track down the thief. Elsewhere, Al and Jimmi re-launch the Icon, but have their efforts paid off? Sid tries to help a desperate father who wants more access to his estranged son and is prepared to break the law to make it happen(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a sports lecturer, a customer adviser and a businessman must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton headsa to Pembrokeshire to help a couple with a £325,000 budget find a suitable rural home for their retirement. While in Wales, Alistair also visits an upland farm to discover the challenges and advantages of rearing native south Wales mountain sheep(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, grabbing three items she hopes to transform, sell on and send off to new homes. Reclamation duo the Junk Gypsies are tasked with turning a pair of teak legs into something stunning, while furniture experts the Three Restorers take on a broken desk. The trio of eclectic tip-finds is rounded off with the item Sarah will work on herself - a formica kitchen table and three chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper start their antiques hunt in the New Forest. Raj dives into the history of an outdoor swimming, while Margie discovers an incredible art collection gifted to the people of Bournemouth. The duo then head west to Exeter for auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keegan face a race against the clock as they head to Scotland to tie the knot. With Karen, Mitch, Sonia and Jack in hot pursuit, will the teenage couple make it down the aisle? Back in the Square, as Lisa gets increasingly annoyed at Mel taking over with Louise, Mel celebrates Ruby buying the E20. Jay decides the time has come to step back from the undertaker's, while Ben seizes the opportunity to help Callum earn more money(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Adil Ray assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Surprises are in store when Fleabag attends her godmother's exhibition of erotic art, having managed to acquire a date for the occasion. She finds herself confronted with a declaration of love and a very familiar piece of work, but Claire is notable by her absence. The awkward artistic gathering forces Fleabag to face up to the consequences of her actions - as well as finally revealing what happened to her best friend Boo. Conclusion of the comedy series, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.desc: Consumer series focusing on online dating scams(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: New findings by the Deafness Cognition and Language project that show people born deaf can use their auditory cortex for working memory. With voiceover(n)

With more than 100 people to feed, Sam and Shauna organise a menu of 10 huge beef briskets, barbecued in giant cinder-block smoker, built with the help of the community(n)

programme.desc: Paul Martin and Danny Sebastian help locals in Monks Kirby, Warwickshire, by arranging a sale to raise funds for Carl Hodgetts, who has spina bifida(n)

programme.desc: A visit to Exeter Livestock Market, the biggest agricultural auction in the region, where a team of auctioneers can sell more than 3,000 animals in about four hours(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Billy Connolly sets out to fill in the gaps in his own family history. Starting in Glasgow, the city of Billy's birth, he hopes to find out more about his mother's relatives. He believes they hailed from Ireland, but records concerning his great-great-grandmother only deepen the mystery. Billy also heads to India, where he unearths details of the parts two of his ancestors played in historical events(n)

programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.desc: Johnny Ball, Suzi Ruffell, Nina Wadia and Danny Wallace test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Guests join Andrew Neil for interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.desc: Paul Murton explores lochs close to the densely populated Central Belt, beginning on the banks of the Lake of Menteith and visiting Loch Ard, Loch Arklet, Loch Katrine and Loch Achray, before ending by climbing Ben Venue in the Trossachs(n)

programme.title: Guilt
programme.desc: Max tries to derail private detective Kenny, a drunken no-hoper who used to work for him, but the case has given the investigator the purpose he needs to get back on track. To make matters worse, Max is also dealing with threats from Walter's neighbour Sheila, who reveals she witnessed something on the night in question. Later, Max's wife Claire's grows suspicious of his actions and cracks begin to show in their marriage. Thriller, starring Mark Bonnar, Jamie Sives, Emun Elliott, Ellie Haddington, Ruth Bradley and Sian Brooke(n)

programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the sixth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine investigate a rural case of murder and moonlighting in Ireland in 1894 resulting in the death of James Donovan. With little evidence, the police rounded up several known criminals from the surrounding area, and John Twiss from Co Kerry, over 16 miles from the murder scene, was tried and convicted. He was hanged in February 1895, protesting his innocence(n)

programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely(n)

programme.desc: The panel oversee more business propositions, with returning Dragon Theo Paphitis joining regulars Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two entrepreneurs seeking funding for a range of drinks go to the lengths of setting up a bar in the studio, while other items up for assessment include a design for a pipe-fitting tool and a range of men's cosmetics. programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where treasures include a diamond ring that was purchased for £1, a 17th-century violin and a unique telegram from Adolf Hitler. A set of iconic weather signs and a valuable princess's dress are among the other items turning up on the day(n)

programme.desc: Atul Kochhar puts three cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make the perfect onion bhaji, choosing one of the amateurs to join him in the final(n)

programme.title: Street Auction
programme.desc: Paul Martin helps raise funds for a deserving member of a local community, using hidden treasures found in their street(n)

programme.desc: A visit to Spalding Plant and Vegetable Auction in Lincolnshire, where as many as 1,000 bags and boxes of vegetables and up to 16,000 plants, shrubs and trees can be sold in a day(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Four decades ago, Neasden-born model and actress Twiggy, then known as Lesley Hornby, found her life transformed when a reporter spotted her photo on the wall of a London hairdresser, and she was dubbed the 'face of the 60s'. Suddenly the 16-year-old was catapulted into life as a supermodel, travelling the world, mixing with some of its biggest stars and eventually appearing in films such as The Boyfriend and The Blues Brothers. In the 100th episode of the genealogy show, Twiggy examines her family's past, where she discovers a history involving strong women and a hint of crime(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the south coast of Devon, the crew of Salcombe lifeboat station are called to two of the highest level of emergencies at sea - a Mayday call to a fisherman pulled to the bottom of the sea ensnared in his own fishing gear, and a devastating fire on a boat 15 miles out in the channel. Meanwhile, in the waters off Anglesey, as they go to the rescue of a father and his 13-year-old son, the crew of Moelfre station uncover a story of survival and heroism(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the first quarter-final, inventing a dish with rice as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Grace Dent and Jimi Famurewa. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: An ambush on a Soho restaurant ends with the Mori brothers on the opposite sides of a frantic gun battle - and prompts a series of flashbacks in which Yuto relives the split-second decisions that brought him and Kenzo to this point. Fourteen months ago, Yuto realised he was destined to always be bad when yakuza boss Fukuhara dispatched his best man, Jiro, to recruit him. But he also realised that unlike his brother, Fukuhara believed in him. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira, Yuko Nakamora and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools presents another selection of music, introducing London grime pioneer Kano, rising jazz stars Nerija and south London singer-songwriter Joy Crookes, making her show debut. Completing the line-up are electro-pop band Metronomy and pianist and broadcaster Jamie Cullum, with tracks from his eighth album, Taller(n)

programme.title: Newsnight

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: Bashar al-Assad is now president and his British-born wife Asma is First Lady, but they face problems on all sides and their regime is accused of killing a political enemy. Asma's in-laws don't like the idea of her being First Lady and want to keep her out of the limelight, an angry US president names Syria as part of the Axis of Evil and the United Nations issue resolutions that Bashar's family want him to ignore. The Assads soon find themselves at the centre of a murder investigation, as one of their political enemies is blown up in a car bombing(n)

programme.title: Abused by the Police

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: An extended edition sees Monty Don add autumn perennials to his borders, show how to take root cuttings and reveal plans for next year when he plants bulbs for spring colour. On her Kent allotment, Frances Tophill catches up on some seasonal sowing and invites a special guest to help her with a small building project. Nick Bailey visits Cambridge University Botanic Garden, where he finds out how and why some plants produce their scent at the end of the day. The programme also returns to the wildlife garden of Kate Bradbury to find out about the lives of solitary bees(n)

programme.desc: Paul Martin helps raise funds for a deserving member of a local community, using hidden treasures found in their street(n)

programme.desc: A visit to the Bala Autumn Sale in Snowdonia, north Wales, which is one of the UK's most important sheep dog auctions. Narrated by Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Warwick Davis starts his research with his paternal grandmother Edith, who led to his big break at the age of 11 when she heard a radio advert. That advert was 'looking for short people to appear in Return of the Jedi', and Warwick was catapulted to stardom, continuing his acting career in the likes of Labyrinth and Willow. He takes a non-judgemental approach as he researches the family line stretching back from Edith, finding humanity and humour in some uncomfortable stories, while on his maternal side, he finds out about a postman who lived a double life(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin visits another location, inviting members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the couples' progress as they prepare for tomorrow night's show. Plus, Gethin Jones reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans and there's a live music performance in the It Takes Two studio(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Johnny Ball, Suzi Ruffell, Nina Wadia and Danny Wallace go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dulwich Hamlet v Carlisle United (Kick-off 7.55pm). Coverage of the first-round tie at Champion Hill. The Hamlet defeated fellow National League South side Havant & Waterlooville in the final qualifying round to reach this stage of the competition for the first time since 1998 and will now look to produce an upset against a Carlisle side that have been struggling for form in League Two. Dan Walker presents, with analysis from Micah Richards and Karen Carney(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Olga Koch, Nigel Ng and Suzi Ruffell and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music programme. Jools and Jamie discuss jazz legend Miles Davis' recently released album Rubberband, recorded in 1985. Jamie also delves into the archive to pick his favourite performance from the show's history and shares some of his current favourite new artists as well as performing a track from his recent album, Taller. Electro-pop band Metronomy, rapper Kano and jazz collective Nerija also perform, while rising south London star Joy Crookes makes her Later debut performing new single Early, joined by star of the burgeoning Dublin hip-hop scene Jafaris(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: Amber Butchart explores the lives of historical figures through the clothes they wore. Here she focuses on the ill-fated French queen, Marie Antoinette, and learns more about her lack of political judgment and her desire to be released from the formality of court life(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy witnesses the impact of Anne Boleyn on Henry VIII's affections, uncovering private moments that were reported by eyewitnesses or recorded in historical documents. The key moments during their marriage are recreated, as Anne falls from favour in court and is eventually executed, despite protestations of innocence regarding the rumours of her infidelity. As soon as Anne is dead, Henry marries Jane Seymour, who fulfils her most important role as Queen - giving birth to the healthy son that Henry has craved for so long(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Dr Bendor Grosvenor finds two anonymous landscape paintings at Birmingham Art Gallery, which he believes may be by the Flemish painter Brueghel(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and architecture of Barcelona, seeking out the Romanesque frescoes that inspired a young Miró and the designs of an engineer who arguably put more of a stamp on the city than its star architect, Antoni Gaudí(n)

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Letters: A Monarch Unveiled
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part programme, biographer AN Wilson continues to explore the personal life of Queen Victoria through her journals and letters, examining the monarch's closest relationships in the four decades following Prince Albert's death. Includes readings by Anna Chancellor(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: David Malone investigates the importance of ocean waves. He discovers they are not made of water, can travel sideways and their sound is created by bubbles, and examines the connection between the life cycles of waves and human beings(n)

programme.title: The Renaissance Unchained
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak returns to Italy to trace the Renaissance from its perceived origins with Giotto and takes a look at the importance of religious narrative in Italian art. He reveals that the majority of Italian painting and sculpture in the 14th and 15th centuries was created to inspire devotion, especially in the work of Piero della Francesca, Michelangelo, Boticelli, Giotto, Leonardo, Raphael and Fra Angelico(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy witnesses the impact of Anne Boleyn on Henry VIII's affections, uncovering private moments that were reported by eyewitnesses or recorded in historical documents. The key moments during their marriage are recreated, as Anne falls from favour in court and is eventually executed, despite protestations of innocence regarding the rumours of her infidelity. As soon as Anne is dead, Henry marries Jane Seymour, who fulfils her most important role as Queen - giving birth to the healthy son that Henry has craved for so long(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Dr Bendor Grosvenor finds two anonymous landscape paintings at Birmingham Art Gallery, which he believes may be by the Flemish painter Brueghel(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Mark Goodier present the edition first shown on September 29, featuring performances by Sinitta, U2, Hazell Dean, Alexander O'Neal, Duran Duran, Erasure, Whitney Houston, Bananarama, the Hollies and T'Pau(n)

programme.title: London Calling: Cold War Letters
programme.desc: On the 30th anniversary of the fall of the Berlin Wall, this documentary reveals how for more than 25 years, the BBC gave voice to the silenced people of the former communist state of East Germany by inviting them to secretly write in to a radio show called Letters without Signature. Broadcast on the BBC's German Service, the programme gave voice to ordinary citizens who wrote about life under the repressive communist regime(n)

programme.title: The Fall of the Berlin Wall with John Simpson
programme.desc: To mark the 30th anniversary of the collapse of the Berlin Wall on November 9, 1989, John Simpson, the BBC's longest-serving correspondent, goes back to his reports on what he believes to be the most important story he has ever covered. Back then, John thought the even would change the world for the better, for ever. Three decades on, he looks at how history has actually turned out and whether he was wrong to have been so optimistic(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Berlin Wall
programme.desc: Originally shown to mark 20 years since the structure that divided the city was brought down, this documentary hears from the people most closely involved with the horrors it created. Spies, informers, double-agents and interrogators explain their roles in the corruption and brutalisation of the new socialist world the Wall's designers had thought they were forging(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary examining how nuclear power has changed the world in the 70 years since the bombing of Hiroshima. The film is comprised entirely of archive footage, and explores the positive and negative aspects of the atomic age, contrasting protest marches, the Cold War and the Chernobyl and Fukishima disasters with advances in medical science, such as X rays and MRI scanners. With music by Mogwai(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Mark Goodier present the edition first shown on September 29, featuring performances by Sinitta, U2, Hazell Dean, Alexander O'Neal, Duran Duran, Erasure, Whitney Houston, Bananarama, the Hollies and T'Pau(n)

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr focuses on an era of great commercial and manufacturing growth throughout several regions on the continent. Beginning with the revolutionary transformation of North and West Africa, he travels to the shores of the Sahara Desert, where farmers, traders, warriors and nomads turned this region into the crossroads of some of history's most advanced, and wealthiest, civilizations(n)

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr looks at the powerful, cosmopolitan cities that dotted Africa at the time when Europe was in its Middle Ages. From 1000 to 1600, commerce, wealth and prosperity expanded across Africa, building new cities and founding new powerful states that mark this golden age(n)

programme.title: London Calling: Cold War Letters
programme.desc: On the 30th anniversary of the fall of the Berlin Wall, this documentary reveals how for more than 25 years, the BBC gave voice to the silenced people of the former communist state of East Germany by inviting them to secretly write in to a radio show called Letters without Signature. programme.title: It's Only Rock 'n' Roll: Rock 'n' Roll at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of classic rock 'n' roll artists and songs in celebration of the musical genre, Including performances by Jerry Lee Lewis, Dion, Dick Dale and Neil Sedaka. Plus, a selection of songs with rock 'n' roll in the title, such as Tom Petty's Anything That's Rock 'n' Roll, Joan Jett's I love Rock 'n' Roll and Oasis's Rock 'n' Roll Star(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Gary Davies present the edition first aired on October 13, featuring performances by the Beatmasters with PP Arnold, Sabrina, T'Pau, Enya, The Christians, D Mob featuring Gary Haisman, Kim Wilde, Sinitta, Whitney Houston and Bananarama(n)

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound(n)

programme.title: Forever Young - How Rock 'n' Roll Grew Up
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the ways rock 'n' roll stars have coped with growing old after being symbols of rebellion from the genre's roots in the 1950s. The programme uncovers how artists have enjoyed comebacks in the 21st century, and examines what happened to the mantra 'live fast, die young'. Featuring contributions by Iggy Pop, Lemmy, Rick Wakeman, Suggs and Alison Moyet. Narrated by Cherie Lunghi(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the singer's performances, from the start of his career in the 1960s to his appearance on Later with Jools Holland just before he celebrated his 70th birthday in 2010. programme.desc: The Squirrels ask Duggee if they can grow something when he sows seeds, they decide on onions, carrots, cucumber, pumpkins and strawberries(n)

programme.title: Mister Maker's Arty Party
programme.desc: How to make colourful flower pictures with a washing-up brush dipped in colourful paint, and how the famous author Beatrix Potter got lots of arty ideas in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo's mum measures a wall with a 'number snake', which Pablo follows into the Art World(n)

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne visits Olivia and her pet cat Figo. Figo needs an operation to make him better. Rory joins the keepers at a safari park to see how they care for the hooves of an okapi(n)

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: It's the night of the Slurpy Soup Feast, but Wingston can't grow any bulgy beans, so Miss Grizzlesniff orders some whizzy worms to wriggle to the rescue. Can the worms conjure up enough compost to grow the beans and save the feast?(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Finlay and his father go on a bike ride to a nearby loch. They find insects and seaweed, and Dad shows Finlay how to skim stones across the water. Narrated by Rebecca Keatley(n)

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Animated version of an African folk tale about why squirrels gather nuts(n)

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Indigo is showing us how to make a desert from New Zealand called pavlova.(n)

programme.title: Teacup Travels
programme.desc: Great Aunt Lizzie tells the story of Charlotte and the Adventure of the Pig-Shaped Rattle. In the blazing heat of the Greek summer, Charlotte meets a couple from Aristoi journeying to their daughter's house in the country with a gift for a new baby. There is trouble in store and Charlotte goes on an epic trek to help the family with their new arrival(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Alice organises a relaxation class, and Molly helps get people to attend. However, just as everyone starts to relax, Bob and Moira start on some very noisy work(n)

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Melody settles down to listen to some music, and her imagination takes her into the animated story of the princess and the pea(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: Greengrocer Yusuf finds a little star in a delivery from outer space. The star is very sad, so Yusuf and the people of Biggleton do their best to cheer it up. Narrated by Eamonn Holmes(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious(n)

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Osbourne shows the class a new toy, but Timmy has to find a way of letting everyone play with it(n)

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'apple' and goes on a whirlwind word adventure, exploring how apples look and taste. There's a green apple, a red apple and a bobbing apple(n)

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The friends watch a man polishing a suit of armour, but spot that he has dropped the cloth inside the armour(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes ice skating with his friends and Mr Tumble builds a cosy den in his bedroom(n)

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy ballet twirls with the Tiddlytubbies, and the children in Tummy Tales put on a ballet show for their family at home. Fun and games for youngsters with the playful quartet(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo feels 'bleugh', making it hard to choose between the library and the swimming pool(n)

programme.desc: Today it is Welsh Day, and the children in reception are celebrating the Welsh language(n)

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures

programme.title: Swashbuckle

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels are tidying up- its ‘Bring your Grandparents to Squirrel Club Day'. Duggee has his Grandparents badge so he knows what they will like - a nice cup of tea. But on arrival, these grandparents seem more of a lively bunch than Duggee was expecting(n)

programme.title: Tinpo

programme.desc: The monster and his friends go on a rollercoaster adventure and figure out why Stan Tall is in such a spin(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate visits an indoor water park and uses a special waterproof camera and animation to show how a water slide works(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter invites Jeremy Fisher along for a garden raid, the melodramatic frog gives him more than he bargained for(n)

programme.desc: Mr Onion tries to be as merry as Old King Cole, so the friends work together to help him(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride on the Pinky Ponk, but it bumps into a tree and the gazebo. Narrated by Derek Jacobi(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple with a £400,000 budget who want to move to rural Shropshire to improve their young daughter's quality of life and reconnect with family roots. programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A broken coffee table requires expertise to redeem it, bag-maker Neil Wragg uses all of his creativity to rethink an old bicycle and Sarah Moore renovates an old dressing table(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: David Dimbleby travels across the UK in the lead up to the 2019 general election to reveal why it is going to be one of the most unpredictable elections in recent history. David finds a United Kingdom divided, and discovers people's views on Brexit have largely hardened, while party allegiances have weakened, and fury with politicians is rarely far from the surface(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam Frisby signs up social media influencer and body positivity campaigner Chessie King to work on a new collection for In The Style. Adam wants the new collection to be released before the summer season ends, which means the team must work quickly to get their ideas together, design and make the range(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets a couple looking for a sanctuary in the Lancashire countryside for themselves and some donkeys. He also visits the Helmshore Mills Textile Museum(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is joined by Norman Wilkinson and Daniel Heath, who between them revamp a guest bed, a tired armchair and an artist's easel - turning the latter into a vanity unit(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper hunt for items along the West Sussex coast, taking in a Sherman tank, a Jack Russell and a mannequin on their way to the auction in Winchester(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: A live report from Holyhead on the start of this year's Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need, as a team of six young people, supported by Matt Baker, set out on an eight-day journey. The team will travel across Wales for three days before crossing over into England in Oswestry, and then making their way down to the finish line at the BBC Elstree Studios on Friday November 15 during the Children in Need appeal show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany cannot contain her excitement when she learns the register office has a cancellation later that day so she, Keegan and Bernadette make final preparations for a trip to Scotland. But unknown to them, Louise takes great pleasure in telling Karen their plans. Ruby is thrilled when Sharon finally signs over the E20, and Ian is determined to make his campaign for Walford councillor a success - unknown Iqra is plotting against him. Pam arrives back in Walford, so Jay and Lola hatch a plan to surprise Ben, who is thrilled to see her(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic boxing bronze medallist Anthony Ogogo, five-time Paralympic champion Hannah Cockroft, former England rugby union player Ugo Monye and Formula One driver Jolyon Palmer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Highlights of the fifth annual event in which the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy and is attempting to walk twice as far as he has ever walked in his life(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: Officers prepare for a visit from President Trump - and for keeping in line the protestors who are out in force. In south London, a 38-year-old man is violently attacked on a bus and rushed to hospital in a critical condition. Due to the serious nature of the attack, the murder detectives are brought in to assist - and as they scour CCTV footage, they see the suspect leaving a vital clue as he makes his getaway. Response officers Ben and Tom assist in the eviction of a traveller from a supermarket car park in Croydon(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Glasgow, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present a combination of gossip and serious analysis from Westminster and Brussels(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate how technology enables people to be caught out by elaborate online romance fraud(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A railway worker's £300,000 personal injury claim for a broken thumb hits the buffers when undercover filming proves he is taking his insurers for a ride, and a fraudulent claim for a mobile phone lost in a river sinks like a stone when the handset resurfaces in a recycling shop. Ore Oduba presents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An elderly man's home is broken into and expensive watches of great sentimental value are taken. Thankfully, a neighbour's security cameras video the thief in the act. Plus, a prolific burglar targets businesses across a whole area, including a pizza shop, a pub and a chippy. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma is furious to discover Heston's memorial bench has been stolen and resolves to track down the thief. Elsewhere, Al and Jimmi re-launch the Icon, but have their efforts paid off? Sid tries to help a desperate father who wants more access to his estranged son and is prepared to break the law to make it happen(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a sports lecturer, a customer adviser and a businessman must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton headsa to Pembrokeshire to help a couple with a £325,000 budget find a suitable rural home for their retirement. While in Wales, Alistair also visits an upland farm to discover the challenges and advantages of rearing native south Wales mountain sheep(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, grabbing three items she hopes to transform, sell on and send off to new homes. Reclamation duo the Junk Gypsies are tasked with turning a pair of teak legs into something stunning, while furniture experts the Three Restorers take on a broken desk. The trio of eclectic tip-finds is rounded off with the item Sarah will work on herself - a formica kitchen table and three chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper start their antiques hunt in the New Forest. Raj dives into the history of an outdoor swimming, while Margie discovers an incredible art collection gifted to the people of Bournemouth. The duo then head west to Exeter for auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keegan face a race against the clock as they head to Scotland to tie the knot. With Karen, Mitch, Sonia and Jack in hot pursuit, will the teenage couple make it down the aisle? Back in the Square, as Lisa gets increasingly annoyed at Mel taking over with Louise, Mel celebrates Ruby buying the E20. Jay decides the time has come to step back from the undertaker's, while Ben seizes the opportunity to help Callum earn more money(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Adil Ray assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: Wow! Wales promotes genealogy tourism and all of the staff do DNA tests to find out how Welsh they are, while the 'King of Wales' pays a visit from America. Comedy, starring Sally Phillips, with guest appearances by Scott Quinnell and Stuart Milligan(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi and Al realise they have forgotten an important date. Valerie has to think on her feet when she tries to help an elderly patient about to be conned out of her life savings(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets a couple looking for a sanctuary in the Lancashire countryside for themselves and some donkeys. He also visits the Helmshore Mills Textile Museum(n)

programme.desc: Sarah Moore is joined by Norman Wilkinson and Daniel Heath, who between them revamp a guest bed, a tired armchair and an artist's easel - turning the latter into a vanity unit(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper hunt for items along the West Sussex coast, taking in a Sherman tank, a Jack Russell and a mannequin on their way to the auction in Winchester(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: A live report from Holyhead on the start of this year's Rickshaw Challenge for Children in Need, as a team of six young people, supported by Matt Baker, set out on an eight-day journey. The team will travel across Wales for three days before crossing over into England in Oswestry, and then making their way down to the finish line at the BBC Elstree Studios on Friday November 15 during the Children in Need appeal show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany cannot contain her excitement when she learns the register office has a cancellation later that day so she, Keegan and Bernadette make final preparations for a trip to Scotland. But unknown to them, Louise takes great pleasure in telling Karen their plans. Ruby is thrilled when Sharon finally signs over the E20, and Ian is determined to make his campaign for Walford councillor a success - unknown Iqra is plotting against him. Pam arrives back in Walford, so Jay and Lola hatch a plan to surprise Ben, who is thrilled to see her(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of Sainsbury's. After a challenging Christmas, the staff go all out to maximise profits with their flower sales over the Mother's Day weekend. Plans are also made to sell a birthday cake to celebrate the company's 150th year of trading, bringing in Claire Ptak, the creator of Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding cake, for ideas. Plus, last year CEO Mike Coupe announced a possible merger with Asda. Will industry regulators give the company the go-ahead to become the biggest supermarket in the country?(n)

programme.title: Laid Bare
programme.desc: Desk-bound Ryan is 30 years old, loves soft drinks and takeaways and has put on about four stones. Super-fit mum Marie is constantly on the go, but despite her her health and fitness routines, she has trouble sleeping - which probably isn't helped by the 10 cups of coffee she drinks every day. When medical tests reveal how they are each damaging their health, they are given eight weeks to turn their lives around(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Glasgow, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present a combination of gossip and serious analysis from Westminster and Brussels(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate how technology enables people to be caught out by elaborate online romance fraud(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A railway worker's £300,000 personal injury claim for a broken thumb hits the buffers when undercover filming proves he is taking his insurers for a ride, and a fraudulent claim for a mobile phone lost in a river sinks like a stone when the handset resurfaces in a recycling shop. Ore Oduba presents(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: An elderly man's home is broken into and expensive watches of great sentimental value are taken. Thankfully, a neighbour's security cameras video the thief in the act. Plus, a prolific burglar targets businesses across a whole area, including a pizza shop, a pub and a chippy. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma is furious to discover Heston's memorial bench has been stolen and resolves to track down the thief. Elsewhere, Al and Jimmi re-launch the Icon, but have their efforts paid off? Sid tries to help a desperate father who wants more access to his estranged son and is prepared to break the law to make it happen(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a sports lecturer, a customer adviser and a businessman must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton headsa to Pembrokeshire to help a couple with a £325,000 budget find a suitable rural home for their retirement. While in Wales, Alistair also visits an upland farm to discover the challenges and advantages of rearing native south Wales mountain sheep(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits a recycling centre in Walsall, West Midlands, grabbing three items she hopes to transform, sell on and send off to new homes. Reclamation duo the Junk Gypsies are tasked with turning a pair of teak legs into something stunning, while furniture experts the Three Restorers take on a broken desk. The trio of eclectic tip-finds is rounded off with the item Sarah will work on herself - a formica kitchen table and three chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper start their antiques hunt in the New Forest. Raj dives into the history of an outdoor swimming, while Margie discovers an incredible art collection gifted to the people of Bournemouth. The duo then head west to Exeter for auction(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Test Drive
programme.desc: Grado hosts the car-based game show in which three teams from Dumfries answer questions via a specially adapted satnav that will determine their route to a well-known tourist attraction. Correct responses mean they will follow the shortest route, but if they are wrong there will be diversions, wrong turns and miles added to their trip(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany and Keegan face a race against the clock as they head to Scotland to tie the knot. With Karen, Mitch, Sonia and Jack in hot pursuit, will the teenage couple make it down the aisle? Back in the Square, as Lisa gets increasingly annoyed at Mel taking over with Louise, Mel celebrates Ruby buying the E20. Jay decides the time has come to step back from the undertaker's, while Ben seizes the opportunity to help Callum earn more money(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Adil Ray assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to Oscar-winner Olivia Colman and Helena Bonham Carter, who are joining Netflix drama The Crown as the Queen and Princess Margaret respectively. Black Panther star Chadwick Boseman discusses his role in new action thriller 21 Bridges, while comedian and actor Richard Ayoade talks about his book Ayoade on Top. Plus, Niall Horan performs Nice to Meet Ya(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Motorcycling great Carl Fogarty, England netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown, Olympic hockey gold medallist Helen Richardson-Walsh and world champion swimmer Mark Foster join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. Plus, Robert Borthwick presents to the panel his favourite clips, comments, GIFS and antics from the world of social media, whether that's from fans, players, teams or managers(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather, presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste track down a man who made off with a woman's savings, and look at how social media profiles and pictures can be hijacked by criminals(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a Manchester flat, there is another look at a Kent property in Bexleyheath, and Dion Dublin examines a semi-detached Wolverhampton house(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Shocking spelling is the undoing of a chancer attempting to cash in on their travel insurance policy for medical treatment that never took place(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A family suffer a horrific ordeal when masked thieves burst into their home. Plus, a bungling bike thief looking to steal a new set of wheels ends up getting a ride in a police car(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help the red and blue teams find bargains in the antique shops of Dorking in Surrey, while host Anita Manning visits a Roman villa in West Sussex(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi struggles with the aftermath of his mugging. Meanwhile, Al is offered a rare comic from two suspicious window cleaners(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Michael, Laura and Dubem must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple with a £400,000 budget who want to move to rural Shropshire to improve their young daughter's quality of life and reconnect with family roots. While in the county, Alistair becomes an apprentice under the watchful eye of a master craftsman who handmakes horse harnesses and collars, and then puts them to the test on a horse, ploughing a field the Victorian way(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A broken coffee table requires expertise to redeem it, bag-maker Neil Wragg uses all of his creativity to rethink an old bicycle and Sarah Moore renovates an old dressing table(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Margie Cooper head to East Sussex for their next auction, and once again Raj gambles a three-figure sum on an item while Margie tries to be clever with her purchases(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: David Dimbleby travels across the UK in the lead up to the 2019 general election to reveal why it is going to be one of the most unpredictable elections in recent history. David finds a United Kingdom divided, and discovers people's views on Brexit have largely hardened, while party allegiances have weakened, and fury with politicians is rarely far from the surface(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to a top theme park where they are challenged to create and market their own roller coaster. They will need to design and brand their new attraction for the theme park which will be turned into a 4-D ride that they then pitch to the park's top talent. During the design process, miscommunication costs one team dearly, and later, both teams struggle as they pitch their ideas to the panel. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates from the losing team leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: First Time Doctors
programme.desc: Following a group of newly qualified doctors as they joined hospitals in Northern Ireland's Western Health Trust for the first time. In the second episode, the recently qualified doctors work over the busy Christmas period for the first time, with cameras capturing the reality of the pressures facing hospitals during the winter months(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: CEO Adam Frisby signs up social media influencer and body positivity campaigner Chessie King to work on a new collection for In The Style. Adam wants the new collection to be released before the summer season ends, which means the team must work quickly to get their ideas together, design and make the range(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Angela Rippon investigates new methods scientists are searching for to fight bacterial infection, revealing how a growing number of bacterial diseases are becoming resistant to the antibiotics currently in use. She meets a GP on the frontline in the fight against infection, and finds out how people can take part in the effort to keep the 'miracle cure' effective(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: Officers prepare for a visit from President Trump - and for keeping in line the protestors who are out in force. In south London, a 38-year-old man is violently attacked on a bus and rushed to hospital in a critical condition. Due to the serious nature of the attack, the murder detectives are brought in to assist - and as they scour CCTV footage, they see the suspect leaving a vital clue as he makes his getaway. Response officers Ben and Tom assist in the eviction of a traveller from a supermarket car park in Croydon(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. In addition - with more Commons archive - Bercow Bows Out offers the chance to re-live some of his favourite unique phrases, whether he's advising an MP to search for ‘a soothing medicament' or accusing others of ‘chuntering from a sedentary position'(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Over 30 years on, the scale and magnitude of the Piper Alpha disaster continues to shock. Iona Ballantyne's father Bob was a well-known spokesman for survivors and their families, and a delayed response to grief was felt strongly by Iona years after his death. To understand and process her own thoughts and loss she visits other families, who like her, were direct witnesses to the consequences of the disaster and the changes it forced upon them(n)

programme.title: HMS Timbertown
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the experiences of 102 sailors from Lewis who were interned in a PoW camp in the Netherlands for the duration of the First World War. Presenter Angela MacLean retraces the journey the men took to the camp, which became known as HMS Timbertown, and uncovers the realities of their lives there - from the highs of football matches, to the lows of failed escape attempts and execution(n)

programme.desc: Shona has to admit that the wedding is off, until news arrives that fresh evidence means David may be released.
